name: Preference Lists Belong to Bijou
designPattern: Canonical Preference List / Preference Row Component Family
requirements: Settings Belong to the Shell design note
Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: user of a framed Bijou app
userStory.iWant: settings and preference rows to use a clear, reusable Bijou component style
userStory.soThat: the shell feels intentional and the same preference vocabulary can appear consistently across apps and shell surfaces

acceptanceCriteria: Bijou exposes a canonical preference-row / preference-list surface family instead of leaving settings-row polish trapped inside shell-local rendering
The shell settings drawer can render toggle, choice, info, and action rows through that component family
Selected rows remain visually obvious without relying on ad hoc shell-only drawing behavior
Long value labels can stack beneath the label instead of colliding or truncating ambiguously
Descriptions remain readable in compact drawer widths
DOGFOOD can adopt the canonical preference-list family as the first proving surface
The first slice works entirely in memory and does not require persistence
backwardCompatibility: This is an internal and additive design-system promotion step. Existing framed apps remain valid. The shell may continue accepting FrameSettings as the declarative input model while switching its rendering to the canonical preference-list family internally.

inScope: DOGFOOD as the first proving surface
outScope: Arbitrary embedded text fields or editors in preference rows
A general storage layer for app settings
Migrating every shell sidecar to the same component family in one slice

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing tests for canonical preference-row rendering that lock selected-row treatment, toggle and choice affordances, description layout, and stacked-value fallback. Then migrate the shell settings drawer to use that component family and keep DOGFOOD green through docs-preview regressions.
edgeCases: Very narrow drawer widths where the value cannot fit beside the label
Long descriptions under a selected row
Choice rows with long current values
testPlan.fuzzStress: Not a priority in v0. The main risk is canonicalizing the visual contract without regressing the shell UX.
failureCases: The shell keeps bespoke settings-row rendering instead of using the new component family
Long values still collide with labels in narrow drawers
Selected rows become harder to distinguish after the migration
The component family quietly assumes storage or persistence concerns

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~180-350 lines across a new component family, shell adoption, and focused regressions
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(R) for rendering visible preference rows where R is the number of rows in the rendered preference list
